ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "middle_name" TEXT /* Middle name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the customer (e.g., F, M, GY, TGW) */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Income range of the customer */,
  "address_line1" TEXT /* First line of the customer's address */,
  "address_line2" TEXT /* Second line of the customer's address */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer's address */,
  "mobile" TEXT /* Mobile phone number of the customer */,
  "zip_code" INTEGER /* Zip code of the customer's address */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "is_pepf" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the customer is a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) or Family Member or Close Associate (Fg) */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Account status of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer preference */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "marketing_opt_in" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the customer has opted in for marketing communications */,
  "communication_channel_preference" TEXT /* Customer's preferred communication channel (e.g., phone, email, SMS) */,
  "language_preference" TEXT /* Customer's preferred language */,
  "preferred_contact_time" TEXT /* Preferred time for customer contact (e.g., morning, evening) */,
  "preferred_contact_channel" TEXT /* Preferred channel for customer contact (e.g., phone, email) */,
  "data_usage_consent" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the customer has given consent for their data to be used for marketing purposes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_preferences_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., phone call, email, in-person visit) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel used for the interaction (e.g., phone, email, in-person) */,
  "interaction_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the interaction in minutes */,
  "interaction_details" TEXT /* Details of the interaction */,
  "interaction_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction (e.g., resolved, unresolved, pending) */,
  "interaction_result" TEXT /* Result or outcome of the interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "user_type" TEXT /* Type of user (e.g., admin, sales, marketing) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "department_head" TEXT /* Name of the department head */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer whose data was accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_reason" TEXT /* Reason for accessing customer data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the campaign */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "campaign_type" TEXT /* Type of campaign (e.g., promotional, educational) */,
  "target_region" TEXT /* Target region for the campaign */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the campaign (e.g., planned, active, completed) */,
  "campaign_performance" TEXT /* Performance metrics for the campaign (e.g., click-through rate, conversion rate) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_customer_map" (
  "map_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign-customer mapping */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing campaign */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Customer's response to the campaign (e.g., accepted, declined, pending) */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the customer responded to the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("map_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_customer_map_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_customer_map_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES marketing_campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer feedback */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interaction related to the feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was provided */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Satisfaction rating (1-5, 5 being the highest) */,
  "feedback_type" TEXT /* Type of feedback (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "feedback_category" TEXT /* Category of feedback (e.g., product, service, support) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or additional feedback provided by the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_feedback_interaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("interaction_id") REFERENCES customer_interactions ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
